ddls:
CREATE TABLE "participants" (
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each participant */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the participant */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the participant */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the participant */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the participant */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the participant */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the participant */,
  "consent_date" TEXT /* Date the participant provided consent */,
  "participation_date" TEXT /* Date the participant participated in the study */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the participant */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level of the participant */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the participant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "blocks" (
  "block_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each block */,
  "block_name" TEXT /* Name of the block */,
  "block_description" TEXT /* Description of the block */,
  "num_sequences" INTEGER /* Number of sequences in the block */,
  "block_type" TEXT /* Type of block (e.g., training, test) */,
  "block_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the block in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("block_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sequences" (
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sequence */,
  "block_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the block the sequence belongs to */,
  "sequence_name" TEXT /* Name of the sequence */,
  "sequence_order" INTEGER /* Order of the sequence within the block */,
  "sequence_type" TEXT /* Type of sequence (e.g., random, fixed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sequences_block_id FOREIGN KEY ("block_id") REFERENCES blocks ("block_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "items" (
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each item */,
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sequence the item belongs to */,
  "item_name" TEXT /* Name of the item */,
  "item_order" INTEGER /* Order of the item within the sequence */,
  "item_type" TEXT /* Type of item (e.g., image, text) */,
  "itemdifficulty" INTEGER /* Difficulty level of the item (e.g., easy, hard) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_items_sequence_id FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id") REFERENCES sequences ("sequence_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant */,
  "block_id" INTEGER /* ID of the block */,
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sequence */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the item */,
  "button_press" INTEGER /* Button press response by the participant */,
  "reaction_time" REAL /* Reaction time in milliseconds */,
  "accuracy" INTEGER /* Accuracy of the response (0 or 1) */,
  "confidence_level" INTEGER /* Confidence level of the participant (scale: 1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_participant_id FOREIGN KEY ("participant_id") REFERENCES participants ("participant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_sequence_id FOREIGN KEY ("sequence_id") REFERENCES sequences ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_block_id FOREIGN KEY ("block_id") REFERENCES blocks ("block_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("item_id") REFERENCES items ("item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "experiment_design" (
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each experiment */,
  "experiment_name" TEXT /* Name of the experiment */,
  "experiment_description" TEXT /* Description of the experiment */,
  "num_blocks" INTEGER /* Number of blocks in the experiment */,
  "num_sequences" INTEGER /* Number of sequences in the experiment */,
  "num_items" INTEGER /* Number of items in the experiment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "experiment_participants" (
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the experiment */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the participant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experiment_id", "participant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "experiment_blocks" (
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the experiment */,
  "block_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the block */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experiment_id", "block_id")
)
column_names: Date of birth of the participant
Date the participant participated in the study
Number of sequences in the block
Duration of the block in seconds
Reference to the block the sequence belongs to
Order of the sequence within the block
Reference to the sequence the item belongs to
